metadata.name: Sidebar
metadata.description: Site-wide left-rail navigation. Renders a persistent vertical column that lists every documentation route grouped by category (Learn / Guides / Concepts / Reference / Platforms / Tools). Each section is a collapsible group with a leading category icon (rendered via the standard `Image` type against docs/screens/images/icon_<sectionId>.svg); under the header an `<ul>` of links where each row carries aria-current='page' when the row URL matches the active route. Consumer (Chrome screen ViewModel) seeds the items catalog, owns the collapse state, and handles the toggle / link-tap events. Component stays presentational — it renders what it's given and fires events; all state lives outside.

items.name: items
items.description: Ordered list of nav sections. Each section is a SidebarSection with id, label, iconName, and a list of entries. The consumer seeds this from a static catalog keyed by URL.

items.name: activeUrl
items.description: Current route pathname (e.g. '/learn/installation'). The component marks the row whose url matches with aria-current='page' and an accent styling.

items.name: collapsedIds
items.description: Ids of sections currently collapsed. Sections not in this list render expanded. Default: empty (all expanded).

items.name: mobileOpen
items.description: Under 1024px viewports the sidebar is hidden by default; flip this to true to slide it in as a drawer. CSS handles the transform; the component sets data-mobile-open accordingly.

exposedEvents.name: onToggleSection
exposedEvents.description: Fires when a section header is tapped. Consumer toggles membership of id in its collapsedIds state.

exposedEvents.name: onLinkTap
exposedEvents.description: Fires after a navigation link is clicked. No preventDefault — the underlying <a> still navigates. Consumer uses this to close the mobile drawer on tap.

dataFlow.diagram:
flowchart LR
    VM[ChromeViewModel] -- items/activeUrl/collapsedIds/mobileOpen --> Sidebar
    Sidebar -- onToggleSection(id) --> VM
    Sidebar -- onLinkTap(url) --> VM

properties.name: id
properties.description: Stable section id ('learn', 'guides', 'concepts', 'reference', 'platforms', 'tools'). Also matches the icon basename: iconName='learn' → /images/icon_learn.svg.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Display label (already resolved through StringManager by the VM).

properties.name: iconName
properties.description: SVG basename under docs/screens/images/ WITHOUT the icon_ prefix. Component prepends internally.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Localized title (resolved by VM, not by the component).

properties.name: url
properties.description: Navigation target; must match a Next.js App Router route.

properties.name: platforms
properties.description: Optional platform scope. When set to a subset of 'ios'|'android'|'web', the row renders small trailing pills (e.g. iOS / Android) to warn readers the page is platform-specific. Omit (undefined) for cross-platform content — no pills render. Component treats missing as undefined; do not pass empty [] unless you explicitly want a no-pill render signalling 'no platforms'.

notes: Icon rendering uses CSS `mask-image: url(/images/icon_.svg); background-color: currentColor` so the active / inactive icon colour can flip via CSS `color`. Direct would ignore currentColor.
Component self-localizes the outer `aria-label` via StringManager.getString('chrome_sidebar_aria_label') — no prop needed. Consumers can override via the standard aria-label pass-through if desired.
All state is external. Component does NOT read localStorage, does NOT persist anything. Persistence is the ChromeViewModel's concern.
Responsive behavior is CSS-driven. Under 1024px the sidebar translates off-screen by default; setting mobileOpen=true slides it in. Consumer triggers this via the top-bar menu button.
Every prop must be bound (`@{name}`). Array + String + Bool + event props all flow through the standard rjui binding pipeline.

Entry-level `platforms` is optional and backward-compatible. When absent, rows render the way they always have (no pills). Pill labels are resolved through StringManager keys `sidebar_platform_ios` / `sidebar_platform_android` / `sidebar_platform_web` so they localize with the rest of the UI.

relatedFiles.description: Ruby converter; scaffolded via `jui g converter`, then hand-adjusted for add_viewmodel_data_prefix and event-prop emission (mirror the table_of_contents_converter.rb pattern).
12 SVG icons. The 6 needed by this component: icon_learn, icon_guides, icon_concepts, icon_reference, icon_platforms, icon_tools.
